ocr.text: Time/dose phenylephrine given
Total: 1800 mcg/ml
S.NO.  Gene Name  Function
1  MYCBP 113, JAG1 114, MSL1 11, PPARdelta 110,  Cell transformation
2  CCND1 42, 43, c-myc 44, ΔNp63 45, MITF 46, LBH 47, survivin 48 and c-Jun 49, fra-1(Fosl1) 49, FGF18 107, Hath1 [108], MET 109, FGF9 112  Cell growth, Proliferation and survival
3  MMP2 51, MMP9 51, MMP-7 52, MMP26 54, VEGF 48, TIAM1112, TWIST1 115, SNAI2 57, ZEB1116, S100A4 58, uPAR 49  Migration, Invasion, EMT
4  CD44 104, VEGF 48, BMP4 106, Ephb 105, GREM1 110, EDN1103  Progression, Angiogenesis and Niche establishment
5  CD44 104, HTERT 117, NANOG 117, OCT4 118  Cancer stem cells
At 19:48, 200 mcg/ml of phenylephrine was administered, followed by another 200 mcg/ml at 20:00. The dosage was increased
to 400 mcg/ml at both 20:15 and 20:30, with the same amount given again at 20:45, culminating in a total administration of
1800 mcg/ml. In Table 2, the first row, S.NO. 1 lists the genes MYCBP 113, JAG1 114, MSL1 11, and PPARdelta 110,
associated with cell transformation. The second row, S.NO. 2, includes CCND1 42, 43, c-myc 44, ΔNp63 45, MITF 46, LBH
47, survivin 48, c-Jun 49, fra-1 (Fosl1) 49, FGF18 107, Hath1 [108], MET 109, and FGF9 112, indicating functions related to
cell growth, proliferation, and survival. S.NO. 3 features MMP2 51, MMP9 51, MMP-7 52, MMP26 54, VEGF 48, TIAM1112,
TWIST1 115, SNAI2 57, ZEB1116, S100A4 58, and uPAR 49, which play roles in migration, invasion, and epithelial-
mesenchymal transition (EMT). The fourth row, S.NO. 4, consists of CD44 104, VEGF 48, BMP4 106, Ephb 105, GREM1 110,
and EDN1103, focusing on progression, angiogenesis, and niche establishment. Finally, S.NO. 5 lists CD44 104, HTERT 117,
NANOG 117, and OCT4 118, relevant to cancer stem cells.
